# Wrapped Trust | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Functions for wrapping and unwrapping native TRUST tokens.

wrappedTrustDeposit[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#wrappedtrustdeposit "Direct link to wrappedTrustDeposit")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Deposit native TRUST to receive wrapped TRUST.

### Parameters[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

| Parameter | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| config | `WriteConfig` | Contract address, publicClient, walletClient | Yes |
| value | `bigint` | Amount of TRUST to wrap | Yes |

### Returns[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#returns "Direct link to Returns")

    Promise<Hash> // Transaction hash

### Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#example "Direct link to Example")

    import { wrappedTrustDeposit, getContractAddressFromChainId } from '@0xintuition/protocol'import { parseEther } from 'viem'const wrappedTrustAddress = getContractAddressFromChainId('WrappedTrust', chainId)const txHash = await wrappedTrustDeposit(  { address: wrappedTrustAddress, walletClient, publicClient },  { args: [], value: parseEther('10') })console.log('Wrapped 10 TRUST:', txHash)

* * *

wrappedTrustWithdraw[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#wrappedtrustwithdraw "Direct link to wrappedTrustWithdraw")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Withdraw wrapped TRUST to receive native TRUST.

### Parameters[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Parameter | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| config | `WriteConfig` | Contract address, publicClient, walletClient | Yes |
| args | `[bigint]` | Amount of wrapped TRUST to unwrap | Yes |

### Returns[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

    Promise<Hash> // Transaction hash

### Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#example-1 "Direct link to Example")

    import { wrappedTrustWithdraw } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const txHash = await wrappedTrustWithdraw(  { address: wrappedTrustAddress, walletClient, publicClient },  { args: [parseEther('5')] })console.log('Unwrapped 5 TRUST:', txHash)

* * *

Standard ERC20 Functions[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#standard-erc20-functions "Direct link to Standard ERC20 Functions")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Wrapped TRUST implements standard ERC20 functions:

### balanceOf[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#balanceof "Direct link to balanceOf")

    // Using viem's readContractimport { readContract } from 'viem'import { WrappedTrustAbi } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const balance = await readContract(publicClient, {  address: wrappedTrustAddress,  abi: WrappedTrustAbi,  functionName: 'balanceOf',  args: [userAddress],})console.log('Balance:', formatEther(balance))

### totalSupply[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#totalsupply "Direct link to totalSupply")

    const totalSupply = await readContract(publicClient, {  address: wrappedTrustAddress,  abi: WrappedTrustAbi,  functionName: 'totalSupply',})console.log('Total supply:', formatEther(totalSupply))

### allowance[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#allowance "Direct link to allowance")

    const allowance = await readContract(publicClient, {  address: wrappedTrustAddress,  abi: WrappedTrustAbi,  functionName: 'allowance',  args: [ownerAddress, spenderAddress],})console.log('Allowance:', formatEther(allowance))

### approve[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#approve "Direct link to approve")

    import { writeContract } from 'viem'const txHash = await writeContract(walletClient, {  address: wrappedTrustAddress,  abi: WrappedTrustAbi,  functionName: 'approve',  args: [spenderAddress, parseEther('100')],})console.log('Approved:', txHash)

* * *

Complete Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#complete-example "Direct link to Complete Example")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    import {  wrappedTrustDeposit,  wrappedTrustWithdraw,  getContractAddressFromChainId,  WrappedTrustAbi,} from '@0xintuition/protocol'import { parseEther, formatEther, readContract } from 'viem'const wrappedTrustAddress = getContractAddressFromChainId('WrappedTrust', chainId)// Check native balanceconst nativeBalance = await publicClient.getBalance({ address: account.address })console.log('Native TRUST:', formatEther(nativeBalance))// Wrap TRUSTconst wrapAmount = parseEther('10')const wrapTx = await wrappedTrustDeposit(  { address: wrappedTrustAddress, walletClient, publicClient },  { args: [], value: wrapAmount })console.log('Wrapped:', wrapTx)// Check wrapped balanceconst wrappedBalance = await readContract(publicClient, {  address: wrappedTrustAddress,  abi: WrappedTrustAbi,  functionName: 'balanceOf',  args: [account.address],})console.log('Wrapped TRUST:', formatEther(wrappedBalance))// Unwrap halfconst unwrapAmount = parseEther('5')const unwrapTx = await wrappedTrustWithdraw(  { address: wrappedTrustAddress, walletClient, publicClient },  { args: [unwrapAmount] })console.log('Unwrapped:', unwrapTx)

* * *

See Also[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#see-also "Direct link to See Also")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Configuration](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration)
    

*   [wrappedTrustDeposit](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#wrappedtrustdeposit)
    *   [Parameters](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#parameters)

    *   [Example](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/wrapped-trust/overview#example-1)

# Nested Triples | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

One of Intuition's most powerful features is that Triples can reference other Triples, effectively nesting statements to provide context or provenance. Intuition supports using a Triple itself as a Subject or Object in another Triple.

Understanding Nested Triples[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#understanding-nested-triples "Direct link to Understanding Nested Triples")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Think of it as linguistic compression—instead of repeatedly expressing complex relationships, you create them once and reference them by ID. A Triple about "Alice's employment at a specific company with particular conditions" becomes a single referenceable unit.

That unit can then be used in higher-order statements about employment trends, verification claims, or temporal changes. This creates a hierarchy of meaning where complex ideas are built from simpler ones, yet each level remains independently addressable and verifiable.

Example: Disputing a Claim[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#example-disputing-a-claim "Direct link to Example: Disputing a Claim")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Consider an initial Triple:

    [Bob] -- [isFriendOf] --> [Alice]

If Alice disagrees with this claim, we can create a nested Triple:

    [Alice] -- [disproves] --> (Bob isFriendOf Alice)

Here, the Object of the second Triple is not an Atom but a reference to the first Triple (identified by its Triple ID). Alice is essentially asserting that the friendship claim is false.

Adding Evidence & Sources[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#adding-evidence--sources "Direct link to Adding Evidence & Sources")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can link Triples to evidence or citations:

    [Triple X] -- [basedOn] --> [Document Y]

This ability to compose Triples into higher-order statements gives the graph a fractal quality—small facts build into bigger facts, and complex relationships can be broken down into simpler ones.

Linguistic Compression[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#linguistic-compression "Direct link to Linguistic Compression")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The result is an extraordinarily efficient knowledge transmission system. Rather than sending verbose descriptions, systems can exchange compact Triple IDs that expand into rich, contextual information.

Each ID carries with it not just data, but the entire graph of relationships, evidence, and community consensus that supports it.

Use Cases[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#use-cases "Direct link to Use Cases")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Temporal Context[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#temporal-context "Direct link to Temporal Context")

    [[Alice works at Company X]] -- [valid from] --> [2024-01-01][[Alice works at Company X]] -- [valid until] --> [2024-12-31]

### Provenance Tracking[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#provenance-tracking "Direct link to Provenance Tracking")

    [[Ethereum is secure]] -- [claimed by] --> [Security Firm Y][[Ethereum is secure]] -- [verified on] --> [2024-01-15]

### Meta-Information[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#meta-information "Direct link to Meta-Information")

    [[Product X is good]] -- [confidence level] --> [0.85][[Product X is good]] -- [number of attestors] --> [150]

* * *

Next Steps[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#next-steps "Direct link to Next Steps")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Signal Fundamentals](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/signals/fundamentals)
     - Learn how the community validates nested claims

     - Review best practices for Triple design

*   [Understanding Nested Triples](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#understanding-nested-triples)

*   [Adding Evidence & Sources](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#adding-evidence--sources)
    
*   [Linguistic Compression](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#linguistic-compression)
    
*   [Use Cases](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#use-cases)
    *   [Temporal Context](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-concepts/primitives/Triples/nested-triples#temporal-context)

# Use Variables | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/variables#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Always use variables for dynamic values instead of hardcoding.

Anti-Pattern[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/variables#anti-pattern "Direct link to Anti-Pattern")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    # BAD: Hardcoding valuesquery {  atoms(where: { type: { _eq: Person } }) {    term_id    label  }}

Best Practice[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/variables#best-practice "Direct link to Best Practice")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    # GOOD: Using variablesquery GetAtomsByType($type: atom_type!) {  atoms(where: { type: { _eq: $type } }) {    term_id    label  }}

**Variables:**

    {  "type": "Person"}

Benefits[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/variables#benefits "Direct link to Benefits")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Reusability**: Same query, different values
2.  **Type safety**: GraphQL validates variable types
3.  **Security**: Prevents injection attacks
4.  **Caching**: Better query plan caching

*   [Anti-Pattern](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/variables#anti-pattern)
    
*   [Best Practice](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/variables#best-practice)
    
*   [Benefits](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/variables#benefits)

---

# Real-Time Subscriptions | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/subscriptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Implement real-time position monitoring with subscriptions.

Subscription[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/subscriptions#subscription "Direct link to Subscription")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Query

▶ Run Query

subscription WatchPositions(
  $cursor: \[positions\_stream\_cursor\_input\]!
  $accountId: String
  $batchSize: Int!
) {
  positions\_stream(
    cursor: $cursor
    batch\_size: $batchSize
    where: {
      account\_id: { \_eq: $accountId }
      shares: { \_gt: "0" }
    }
  ) {
    id
    shares
    vault {
      term\_id
      current\_share\_price
    }
  }
}

#### Variables

Click "Run Query" to execute the GraphQL query and see results

Implementation[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/subscriptions#implementation "Direct link to Implementation")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    import { createClient } from 'graphql-ws'const client = createClient({  url: 'wss://mainnet.intuition.sh/v1/graphql'})client.subscribe(  {    query: subscription,    variables: {      cursor: [{ initial_value: { created_at: '2024-12-01T00:00:00Z' }, ordering: 'ASC' }],      accountId: '0x...',      batchSize: 10    }  },  {    next: (data) => {      console.log('Update:', data)      // Update UI with new position data    },    error: (error) => {      console.error('Subscription error:', error)    },    complete: () => {      console.log('Subscription complete')    }  })

*   [Subscription](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/subscriptions#subscription)
    
*   [Implementation](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/subscriptions#implementation)

---

# Social Graph | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/social-graph#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Build a social activity feed from followed accounts.

Query[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/social-graph#query "Direct link to Query")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Query

▶ Run Query

query GetFollowingFeed($address: String!, $limit: Int!) {
  following\_count: following\_aggregate(args: { address: $address }) {
    aggregate {
      count
    }
  }
  positions\_from\_following(
    args: { address: $address }
    limit: $limit
    order\_by: { created\_at: desc }
  ) {
    id
    shares
    created\_at
    account {
      id
      label
      image
    }
    vault {
      term\_id
      term {
        atom { label image }
      }
    }
  }
}

#### Variables

Click "Run Query" to execute the GraphQL query and see results

*   [Query](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/social-graph#query)

---

# Upload Image | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Upload a base64-encoded image. The image is cached and a moderation check is performed. Returns a `CachedImage` with the hosted URL and safety score.

Mutation Structure[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#mutation-structure "Direct link to Mutation Structure")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    mutation UploadImage($image: UploadImageInput!) {  uploadImage(image: $image) {    images {      url      original_url      safe      score      model      created_at    }  }}

Variables[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#variables "Direct link to Variables")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The mutation takes an `image` argument with the `UploadImageInput` type:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `data` | `String` | Yes | Base64-encoded image data |
| `filename` | `String` | Yes | Filename with extension |
| `contentType` | `String` | Yes | MIME type (e.g. `"image/png"`) |

    {  "image": {    "data": "iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAADUlEQVR42mNk+M9QDwADhgGAWjR9awAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==",    "filename": "pixel.png",    "contentType": "image/png"  }}

Response Fields[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#response-fields "Direct link to Response Fields")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Returns `UploadImageFromUrlOutput` containing an `images` array of `CachedImage` objects:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `images` | `[CachedImage!]!` | Array of cached image results |
| `images[].url` | `String!` | Hosted image URL |
| `images[].original_url` | `String!` | Original source URL |
| `images[].safe` | `Boolean!` | Whether the image passed moderation |
| `images[].score` | `jsonb` | Moderation safety score details |
| `images[].model` | `String` | Moderation model used |
| `images[].created_at` | `timestamptz!` | Upload timestamp |

Expected Response[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#expected-response "Direct link to Expected Response")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    {  "data": {    "uploadImage": {      "images": [        {          "url": "https://cdn.example.com/images/abc123.png",          "original_url": "https://cdn.example.com/images/abc123.png",          "safe": true,          "score": null,          "model": null,          "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"        }      ]    }  }}

Use Cases[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#use-cases "Direct link to Use Cases")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### File Input Upload[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#file-input-upload "Direct link to File Input Upload")

Handle file uploads from a form:

    import { GraphQLClient } from 'graphql-request'import { API_URL_PROD } from '@0xintuition/graphql'const client = new GraphQLClient(API_URL_PROD)async function uploadFileInput(file: File) {  const buffer = await file.arrayBuffer()  const base64 = btoa(String.fromCharCode(...new Uint8Array(buffer)))  const mutation = `    mutation UploadImage($image: UploadImageInput!) {      uploadImage(image: $image) {        images {          url          safe        }      }    }  `  const result = await client.request(mutation, {    image: {      data: base64,      filename: file.name,      contentType: file.type    }  })  return result.uploadImage.images[0]}

Related[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#related "Direct link to Related")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Upload Image from URL](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image-from-url)
     - Upload from URL
*   [Upload JSON to IPFS](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-json-to-ipfs)
     - Upload metadata
*   [Pin Thing](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/pin-thing)
     - Create Thing with image

*   [Mutation Structure](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#mutation-structure)
    
*   [Variables](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#variables)
    
*   [Response Fields](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#response-fields)
    
*   [Expected Response](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#expected-response)
    
*   [Use Cases](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#use-cases)
    *   [File Input Upload](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#file-input-upload)
        
*   [Related](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image#related)

# Building User Activity Feeds | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/queries/building-user-activity-feeds#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Learn how to create personalized, real-time activity feeds that track user interactions across the Intuition ecosystem using the GraphQL API.

Understanding the Progression[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/queries/building-user-activity-feeds#understanding-the-progression "Direct link to Understanding the Progression")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Get the whole user activity feed**, including atoms made and positions taken, along with vault details.
2.  **Get recent claims** produced by the user, both atoms (max 10) and triples (max 10) with detailed metadata.
3.  **Get active user positions** ordered by share amount - shows current stakes with vault performance metrics.

Step 1: Get User ActivityStep 2: User Recent ClaimsStep 3: Active User Positions

#### Query

▶ Run Query

query GetUserActivity($userAddress: String!, $limit: Int = 20) {
  atoms(
    where: { creator\_id: { \_eq: $userAddress } }
    order\_by: \[{ created\_at: desc }\]
    limit: $limit
  ) {
    term\_id
    label
    image
    type
    created\_at
    transaction\_hash
  }

  positions(
    where: { account\_id: { \_eq: $userAddress } }
    order\_by: \[{ created\_at: desc }\]
    limit: $limit
  ) {
    id
    shares
    created\_at
    vault {
      term\_id
      current\_share\_price
      market\_cap
      term {
        atom {
          term\_id
          label
          image
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

#### Variables

Click "Run Query" to execute the GraphQL query and see results

*   [Understanding the Progression](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/queries/building-user-activity-feeds#understanding-the-progression)

# Tutorials | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/overview#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Learn by building real applications with Intuition. Each tutorial is a complete, end-to-end guide with working code.

Building Applications[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/overview#building-applications "Direct link to Building Applications")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [Reputation System](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/reputation-system)
[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/overview#reputation-system "Direct link to reputation-system")

Build a developer reputation platform with skills, endorsements, and verifiable credentials.

**You'll learn:**

*   Creating identity atoms from Ethereum addresses
*   Skill attestations with triples
*   Weighted endorsements with signals
*   Querying and displaying reputation data

**Time:** 2-3 hours | **Level:** Intermediate

* * *

### [Curated Lists](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/curated-lists)
[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/overview#curated-lists "Direct link to curated-lists")

Create community-curated, ranked lists where stake determines ranking.

**You'll learn:**

*   Token-curated registries (TCRs)
*   Signal-based ranking algorithms
*   List management and curation
*   Dispute resolution mechanisms

**Time:** 2 hours | **Level:** Intermediate

* * *

### [Social Attestations](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/social-attestations)
[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/overview#social-attestations "Direct link to social-attestations")

Build a decentralized professional network with portable identity.

**You'll learn:**

*   Professional profiles as atoms
*   Connection and recommendation triples
*   Experience verification
*   Cross-platform portability

**Time:** 2-3 hours | **Level:** Intermediate

* * *

### [Fraud Detection](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/fraud-detection)
[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/overview#fraud-detection "Direct link to fraud-detection")

Create a community-driven scam flagging system.

**You'll learn:**

*   Safety claim triples
*   Expert-weighted signals
*   Alert and notification systems
*   False positive handling

**Time:** 2 hours | **Level:** Advanced

* * *

### [Prediction Market](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/prediction-market)
[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/overview#prediction-market "Direct link to prediction-market")

Build a non-resolving forecasting platform.

**You'll learn:**

*   Event and prediction atoms
*   Track record calculation
*   Confidence signals
*   Reputation scoring

**Time:** 2 hours | **Level:** Advanced

* * *

Prerequisites for Tutorials[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/overview#prerequisites-for-tutorials "Direct link to Prerequisites for Tutorials")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before starting these tutorials, make sure you have:

1.  **Development Environment**
    
    *   Node.js 16+ installed
    *   TypeScript knowledge
    *   Basic React familiarity (for UI tutorials)
2.  **Intuition Setup**
    
    *   SDK installed (`npm install @0xintuition/sdk`)
    *   Wallet with testnet ETH
    *   Connected to Intuition Testnet
3.  **Recommended Tools**
    
    *   GraphQL client (like Apollo or urql)
    *   Code editor (VS Code recommended)
    *   Git for cloning example repos

See [Getting Started](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/quick-start/using-the-sdk)
 for setup instructions.

# Optimizing Batch Operations | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/advanced/batch-operations#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

> Coming soon! This tutorial will show how to efficiently create and manage multiple atoms and triples in batches.

Learn how to optimize gas costs and improve performance when creating many atoms, triples, or signals at once.

What You'll Learn[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/advanced/batch-operations#what-youll-learn "Direct link to What You'll Learn")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This tutorial will cover:

*   Batch atom creation
*   Bulk triple creation
*   Multi-sig signal deposits
*   Gas optimization strategies
*   Transaction batching patterns
*   Error handling for batch operations

In the Meantime[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/advanced/batch-operations#in-the-meantime "Direct link to In the Meantime")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See these resources:

*   [SDK Documentation](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-sdk/installation-and-setup)
    
*   [Protocol API Reference](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/multivault/atoms)
    
*   [Performance Best Practices](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/intuition-sdk/quick-start)
    

Quick Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/advanced/batch-operations#quick-example "Direct link to Quick Example")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    // Conceptual example - API may differasync function batchCreateAtoms(atomsData: Array<AtomData>) {  // Create all atoms in one transaction  const result = await multivault.batchCreateAtoms({    atoms: atomsData  })  return result.atomIds}// Create 100 skill atoms efficientlyconst skills = ['TypeScript', 'React', 'Solidity', /* ... */]const atomData = skills.map(skill => ({  type: 'thing',  value: skill}))const atomIds = await batchCreateAtoms(atomData)console.log(`Created ${atomIds.length} atoms in one transaction`)

.

# Oracle Integration | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/advanced/oracle-integration#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

> Coming soon! This tutorial will show how to use Intuition as an oracle for your smart contracts.

Learn how to read Intuition's on-chain data from your smart contracts, enabling trust-weighted decision making and reputation-gated access.

What You'll Learn[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/advanced/oracle-integration#what-youll-learn "Direct link to What You'll Learn")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This tutorial will cover:

*   Reading atom and triple data from contracts
*   Querying vault metrics (market cap, positions)
*   Implementing reputation-gated functions
*   Using signals for weighted voting
*   Building trust-based access control
*   Real-time oracle updates

Use Cases[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/advanced/oracle-integration#use-cases "Direct link to Use Cases")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Reputation-gated DAOs** - Only allow voting from trusted accounts
*   **Trust-weighted governance** - Weight votes by reputation
*   **Safety checks** - Query fraud detection data before interacting with contracts
*   **Expert verification** - Require attestations from qualified users
*   **Dynamic access control** - Grant permissions based on on-chain reputation

In the Meantime[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/advanced/oracle-integration#in-the-meantime "Direct link to In the Meantime")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See these resources:

*   [Protocol Smart Contracts](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/overview)
    
*   [Multivault Contract API](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/multivault/atoms)
    
*   [Solidity Integration Guide](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/overview)
    

Quick Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/advanced/oracle-integration#quick-example "Direct link to Quick Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    // Conceptual exampleinterface IIntuitionMultivault {  function getVaultAssets(bytes32 vaultId) external view returns (uint256);  function getPosition(bytes32 vaultId, address account) external view returns (uint256);}contract ReputationGatedDAO {  IIntuitionMultivault public intuition;  bytes32 public reputationVaultId;  uint256 public minimumReputation;  function vote(uint256 proposalId, bool support) external {    // Check caller's reputation    uint256 reputation = intuition.getPosition(reputationVaultId, msg.sender);    require(      reputation >= minimumReputation,      "Insufficient reputation to vote"    );    // Weight vote by reputation    _castVote(proposalId, support, reputation);  }}

Get Notified[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/tutorials/advanced/oracle-integration#get-notified "Direct link to Get Notified")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Want to be notified when this tutorial is ready? Join our [Discord](https://discord.gg/RgBenkX4mx)
.

# Epochs & Utilization | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

The Intuition Protocol operates on an **epoch-based system** for tracking bonding rewards and vault utilization. Epochs are fixed time periods that determine reward distribution.

What are Epochs?[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#what-are-epochs "Direct link to What are Epochs?")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An epoch is a fixed time period used to:

*   Track vault utilization across the protocol
*   Calculate and distribute bonding rewards
*   Measure user activity and participation
*   Determine APY and reward eligibility

Epoch Mechanics[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#epoch-mechanics "Direct link to Epoch Mechanics")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Current Epoch[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#current-epoch "Direct link to Current Epoch")

Get the active epoch number:

    import { multiVaultCurrentEpoch } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const epoch = await multiVaultCurrentEpoch({ address, publicClient })console.log('Current epoch:', epoch)

### Epoch Configuration[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#epoch-configuration "Direct link to Epoch Configuration")

Query epoch parameters from TrustBonding contract:

    import {  trustBondingEpochLength,  trustBondingEpochsPerYear,  getContractAddressFromChainId,} from '@0xintuition/protocol'const bondingAddress = getContractAddressFromChainId('TrustBonding', chainId)// Get epoch durationconst epochLength = await trustBondingEpochLength({  address: bondingAddress,  publicClient,})console.log('Epoch length (seconds):', epochLength)// Get epochs per yearconst epochsPerYear = await trustBondingEpochsPerYear({  address: bondingAddress,  publicClient,})console.log('Epochs per year:', epochsPerYear)

### Epoch at Timestamp[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#epoch-at-timestamp "Direct link to Epoch at Timestamp")

Get the epoch number for a specific time:

    import { trustBondingEpochAtTimestamp } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const timestamp = BigInt(Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000))const epoch = await trustBondingEpochAtTimestamp(  { address: bondingAddress, publicClient },  { args: [timestamp] })

### Epoch End Time[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#epoch-end-time "Direct link to Epoch End Time")

Get when an epoch ends:

    import { trustBondingEpochTimestampEnd } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const endTime = await trustBondingEpochTimestampEnd(  { address: bondingAddress, publicClient },  { args: [epochNumber] })const endDate = new Date(Number(endTime) * 1000)console.log('Epoch ends:', endDate)

Utilization Tracking[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#utilization-tracking "Direct link to Utilization Tracking")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Utilization** measures vault activity and influences reward distribution. Higher utilization = higher rewards.

### Total Utilization[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#total-utilization "Direct link to Total Utilization")

Get system-wide utilization for an epoch:

    import { multiVaultGetTotalUtilizationForEpoch } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const totalUtil = await multiVaultGetTotalUtilizationForEpoch(  { address, publicClient },  { args: [epochNumber] })

### User Utilization[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#user-utilization "Direct link to User Utilization")

Get a user's total utilization across all vaults:

    import { multiVaultGetUserUtilizationForEpoch } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const userUtil = await multiVaultGetUserUtilizationForEpoch(  { address, publicClient },  { args: [userAddress, epochNumber] })

### Vault-Specific Utilization[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#vault-specific-utilization "Direct link to Vault-Specific Utilization")

Get a user's utilization in a specific vault:

    import { multiVaultGetUserUtilizationInEpoch } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const vaultUtil = await multiVaultGetUserUtilizationInEpoch(  { address, publicClient },  { args: [userAddress, vaultId, epochNumber] })

### Last Active Epoch[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#last-active-epoch "Direct link to Last Active Epoch")

Get when a user was last active:

    import { multiVaultGetUserLastActiveEpoch } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const lastEpoch = await multiVaultGetUserLastActiveEpoch(  { address, publicClient },  { args: [userAddress] })

Utilization and Rewards[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#utilization-and-rewards "Direct link to Utilization and Rewards")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Utilization directly impacts reward distribution:

    import {  trustBondingGetUserApy,  trustBondingGetSystemApy,  trustBondingGetUserRewardsForEpoch,} from '@0xintuition/protocol'// Get user APY (based on utilization)const userApy = await trustBondingGetUserApy(  { address: bondingAddress, publicClient },  { args: [userAddress] })// Get system-wide APYconst systemApy = await trustBondingGetSystemApy({  address: bondingAddress,  publicClient,})// Get rewards for specific epochconst rewards = await trustBondingGetUserRewardsForEpoch(  { address: bondingAddress, publicClient },  { args: [userAddress, epochNumber] })

Utilization Ratios[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#utilization-ratios "Direct link to Utilization Ratios")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### System Utilization Ratio[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#system-utilization-ratio "Direct link to System Utilization Ratio")

Overall protocol utilization:

    import { trustBondingGetSystemUtilizationRatio } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const systemRatio = await trustBondingGetSystemUtilizationRatio({  address: bondingAddress,  publicClient,})

### Personal Utilization Ratio[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#personal-utilization-ratio "Direct link to Personal Utilization Ratio")

User's relative utilization:

    import { trustBondingGetPersonalUtilizationRatio } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const personalRatio = await trustBondingGetPersonalUtilizationRatio(  { address: bondingAddress, publicClient },  { args: [userAddress] })

Epoch Lifecycle Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#epoch-lifecycle-example "Direct link to Epoch Lifecycle Example")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    // 1. Get current epoch infoconst currentEpoch = await multiVaultCurrentEpoch({ address, publicClient })const previousEpoch = await trustBondingPreviousEpoch({  address: bondingAddress,  publicClient,})// 2. Check epoch timingconst epochEnd = await trustBondingEpochTimestampEnd(  { address: bondingAddress, publicClient },  { args: [currentEpoch] })// 3. Get utilization for previous epochconst totalUtil = await multiVaultGetTotalUtilizationForEpoch(  { address, publicClient },  { args: [previousEpoch] })// 4. Check user rewardsconst userRewards = await trustBondingGetUserRewardsForEpoch(  { address: bondingAddress, publicClient },  { args: [userAddress, previousEpoch] })// 5. Get emissions for epochconst emissions = await trustBondingEmissionsForEpoch(  { address: bondingAddress, publicClient },  { args: [previousEpoch] })

Common Use Cases[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#common-use-cases "Direct link to Common Use Cases")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Track Reward Eligibility[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#track-reward-eligibility "Direct link to Track Reward Eligibility")

    const lastActive = await multiVaultGetUserLastActiveEpoch(  { address, publicClient },  { args: [userAddress] })const currentEpoch = await multiVaultCurrentEpoch({ address, publicClient })if (lastActive < currentEpoch) {  console.log('User was active in previous epochs')}

### Calculate Historical Utilization[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#calculate-historical-utilization "Direct link to Calculate Historical Utilization")

    const historicalEpochs = [100n, 101n, 102n]for (const epoch of historicalEpochs) {  const util = await multiVaultGetUserUtilizationForEpoch(    { address, publicClient },    { args: [userAddress, epoch] }  )  console.log(`Epoch ${epoch}: ${util} utilization`)}

### Monitor Real-Time Activity[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#monitor-real-time-activity "Direct link to Monitor Real-Time Activity")

    const checkActivity = async () => {  const epoch = await multiVaultCurrentEpoch({ address, publicClient })  const util = await multiVaultGetUserUtilizationForEpoch(    { address, publicClient },    { args: [userAddress, epoch] }  )  return { epoch, utilization: util }}// Poll every minutesetInterval(async () => {  const activity = await checkActivity()  console.log('Current activity:', activity)}, 60000)

Related Functions[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/core-concepts/epochs#related-functions "Direct link to Related Functions")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [multiVaultCurrentEpoch](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/multivault/epochs-utilization#multivaultcurrentepoch)
     - Get current epoch
*   [multiVaultGetTotalUtilizationForEpoch](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/multivault/epochs-utilization#multivaultgettotalutilizationforepoch)
     - Get total utilization
*   [trustBondingGetUserApy](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/trust-bonding/rewards#trustbondinggetuserapy)
     - Get user APY
*   [Trust Bonding Epochs](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/api-reference/trust-bonding/epochs)
     - Epoch management functions

# Configuration | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

This guide covers how to configure the Protocol package for use with Intuition networks, including client setup, network configuration, and contract address management.

Client Configuration[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#client-configuration "Direct link to Client Configuration")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Protocol package uses two types of configurations depending on the operation:

### ReadConfig (Read-Only Operations)[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#readconfig-read-only-operations "Direct link to ReadConfig (Read-Only Operations)")

For querying data without submitting transactions:

    import type { ReadConfig } from '@0xintuition/protocol'import { createPublicClient, http } from 'viem'import { intuitionTestnet, getMultiVaultAddressFromChainId } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const publicClient = createPublicClient({  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: http(),})const readConfig: ReadConfig = {  address: getMultiVaultAddressFromChainId(intuitionTestnet.id),  publicClient: publicClient,}

### WriteConfig (Transaction Operations)[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#writeconfig-transaction-operations "Direct link to WriteConfig (Transaction Operations)")

For operations that modify blockchain state:

    import type { WriteConfig } from '@0xintuition/protocol'import { createPublicClient, createWalletClient, http } from 'viem'import { privateKeyToAccount } from 'viem/accounts'const account = privateKeyToAccount('0x...')const publicClient = createPublicClient({  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: http(),})const walletClient = createWalletClient({  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: http(),  account,})const writeConfig: WriteConfig = {  address: getMultiVaultAddressFromChainId(intuitionTestnet.id),  publicClient: publicClient,  walletClient: walletClient,}

Network Configuration[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#network-configuration "Direct link to Network Configuration")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Intuition Testnet[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#intuition-testnet "Direct link to Intuition Testnet")

The testnet is ideal for development and testing:

    import { intuitionTestnet } from '@0xintuition/protocol'// Chain Configurationconst testnet = {  id: 13579,  name: 'Intuition Testnet',  nativeCurrency: {    name: 'Testnet TRUST',    symbol: 'tTRUST',    decimals: 18,  },  rpcUrls: {    default: {      http: ['https://testnet.rpc.intuition.systems/http'],      webSocket: ['wss://testnet.rpc.intuition.systems/ws'],    },  },  blockExplorers: {    default: {      name: 'Intuition Testnet Explorer',      url: 'https://testnet.explorer.intuition.systems',    },  },}// Usageconst publicClient = createPublicClient({  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: http(),})

### Intuition Mainnet[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#intuition-mainnet "Direct link to Intuition Mainnet")

For production deployments:

    import { intuitionMainnet } from '@0xintuition/protocol'// Chain Configurationconst mainnet = {  id: 1155,  name: 'Intuition',  nativeCurrency: {    name: 'TRUST',    symbol: 'TRUST',    decimals: 18,  },  rpcUrls: {    default: {      http: ['https://rpc.intuition.systems/http'],      webSocket: ['wss://rpc.intuition.systems/ws'],    },  },  blockExplorers: {    default: {      name: 'Intuition Explorer',      url: 'https://explorer.intuition.systems',    },  },}// Usageconst publicClient = createPublicClient({  chain: intuitionMainnet,  transport: http(),})

Contract Addresses[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#contract-addresses "Direct link to Contract Addresses")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### MultiVault Contract[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#multivault-contract "Direct link to MultiVault Contract")

The MultiVault is the main protocol contract for atoms, triples, and vaults:

    import { getMultiVaultAddressFromChainId, intuitionTestnet, intuitionMainnet } from '@0xintuition/protocol'// Testnetconst testnetAddress = getMultiVaultAddressFromChainId(intuitionTestnet.id)// Returns: '0x2Ece8D4dEdcB9918A398528f3fa4688b1d2CAB91'// Mainnetconst mainnetAddress = getMultiVaultAddressFromChainId(intuitionMainnet.id)// Returns: '0x6E35cF57A41fA15eA0EaE9C33e751b01A784Fe7e'

### Other Protocol Contracts[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#other-protocol-contracts "Direct link to Other Protocol Contracts")

For accessing other contracts like TrustBonding or WrappedTrust:

    import { getContractAddressFromChainId } from '@0xintuition/protocol'// TrustBonding contract (for epoch rewards)const bondingAddress = getContractAddressFromChainId('TrustBonding', intuitionMainnet.id)// Returns: '0x635bBD1367B66E7B16a21D6E5A63C812fFC00617'// WrappedTrust contract (for token wrapping)const wrappedTrustAddress = getContractAddressFromChainId('WrappedTrust', intuitionMainnet.id)// Returns: '0x81cFb09cb44f7184Ad934C09F82000701A4bF672'// Bonding curve contractsconst linearCurve = getContractAddressFromChainId('LinearCurve', intuitionMainnet.id)const progressiveCurve = getContractAddressFromChainId('OffsetProgressiveCurve', intuitionMainnet.id)

#### Available Contracts[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#available-contracts "Direct link to Available Contracts")

*   **`'Trust'`** - Native TRUST token (only on Base: Chain ID 8453)
*   **`'WrappedTrust'`** - Wrapped TRUST token
*   **`'MultiVault'`** - Main protocol contract
*   **`'TrustBonding'`** - Bonding and rewards contract
*   **`'BondingCurveRegistry'`** - Curve registry
*   **`'LinearCurve'`** - Linear bonding curve
*   **`'OffsetProgressiveCurve'`** - Progressive bonding curve

### All Deployments[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#all-deployments "Direct link to All Deployments")

Access all contract addresses directly:

    import { intuitionDeployments } from '@0xintuition/protocol'// Access deployment dataconst allDeployments = intuitionDeployments/*{  MultiVault: { 1155: '0x6E35...', 13579: '0x2Ece...' },  TrustBonding: { 1155: '0x635b...', 13579: '0x75dD...' },  WrappedTrust: { 1155: '0x81cF...', 13579: '0xDE80...' },  BondingCurveRegistry: { ... },  LinearCurve: { ... },  OffsetProgressiveCurve: { ... },}*/

Transport Options[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#transport-options "Direct link to Transport Options")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### HTTP Transport[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#http-transport "Direct link to HTTP Transport")

Standard HTTP connection (recommended for most use cases):

    import { http } from 'viem'const publicClient = createPublicClient({  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: http(),})

### WebSocket Transport[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#websocket-transport "Direct link to WebSocket Transport")

Real-time updates and subscriptions:

    import { webSocket } from 'viem'const publicClient = createPublicClient({  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: webSocket('wss://testnet.rpc.intuition.systems/ws'),})// Subscribe to new blocksconst unwatch = publicClient.watchBlockNumber({  onBlockNumber: (blockNumber) => {    console.log('New block:', blockNumber)  },})

### Custom RPC[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#custom-rpc "Direct link to Custom RPC")

Use a custom RPC endpoint:

    const publicClient = createPublicClient({  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: http('https://your-custom-rpc-endpoint.com'),})

Wallet Configuration[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#wallet-configuration "Direct link to Wallet Configuration")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Private Key Account[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#private-key-account "Direct link to Private Key Account")

For server-side or automated operations:

    import { privateKeyToAccount } from 'viem/accounts'const account = privateKeyToAccount('0x...')const walletClient = createWalletClient({  account,  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: http(),})

### Browser Wallet (MetaMask, etc.)[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#browser-wallet-metamask-etc "Direct link to Browser Wallet (MetaMask, etc.)")

For browser-based applications:

    import { custom } from 'viem'import { createWalletClient } from 'viem'// Browser walletconst walletClient = createWalletClient({  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: custom(window.ethereum),})// Get account from walletconst [account] = await walletClient.getAddresses()

### Using Wagmi (React)[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#using-wagmi-react "Direct link to Using Wagmi (React)")

For React applications using Wagmi:

    import { usePublicClient, useWalletClient } from 'wagmi'import { getMultiVaultAddressFromChainId, multiVaultGetAtomCost } from '@0xintuition/protocol'function MyComponent() {  const publicClient = usePublicClient()  const { data: walletClient } = useWalletClient()  const address = getMultiVaultAddressFromChainId(publicClient.chain.id)  const fetchAtomCost = async () => {    const cost = await multiVaultGetAtomCost({ address, publicClient })    console.log('Atom cost:', cost)  }  return <button onClick={fetchAtomCost}>Get Atom Cost</button>}

Environment Variables[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#environment-variables "Direct link to Environment Variables")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Store sensitive configuration in environment variables:

    # .envPRIVATE_KEY=0x...RPC_URL=https://testnet.rpc.intuition.systems/httpCHAIN_ID=13579

    import { privateKeyToAccount } from 'viem/accounts'import { createPublicClient, createWalletClient, http } from 'viem'import { intuitionTestnet } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const account = privateKeyToAccount(process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as `0x${string}`)const publicClient = createPublicClient({  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: http(process.env.RPC_URL),})const walletClient = createWalletClient({  account,  chain: intuitionTestnet,  transport: http(process.env.RPC_URL),})

Complete Configuration Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#complete-configuration-example "Direct link to Complete Configuration Example")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here's a complete configuration setup for a production application:

    import {  createPublicClient,  createWalletClient,  http,  webSocket,} from 'viem'import { privateKeyToAccount } from 'viem/accounts'import {  intuitionMainnet,  intuitionTestnet,  getMultiVaultAddressFromChainId,  getContractAddressFromChainId,} from '@0xintuition/protocol'// Determine network from environmentconst isProduction = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'const chain = isProduction ? intuitionMainnet : intuitionTestnet// Create account from private keyconst account = privateKeyToAccount(process.env.PRIVATE_KEY as `0x${string}`)// Create public client (for reads)const publicClient = createPublicClient({  chain,  transport: http(),})// Create wallet client (for writes)const walletClient = createWalletClient({  account,  chain,  transport: http(),})// Get contract addressesconst multiVaultAddress = getMultiVaultAddressFromChainId(chain.id)const trustBondingAddress = getContractAddressFromChainId('TrustBonding', chain.id)const wrappedTrustAddress = getContractAddressFromChainId('WrappedTrust', chain.id)// Export configurationexport const config = {  publicClient,  walletClient,  account,  addresses: {    multiVault: multiVaultAddress,    trustBonding: trustBondingAddress,    wrappedTrust: wrappedTrustAddress,  },}

Next Steps[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/getting-started/configuration#next-steps "Direct link to Next Steps")

# Query Performance | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Optimize your GraphQL queries for better performance.

Performance Checklist[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#performance-checklist "Direct link to Performance Checklist")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 1\. Use Indexed Fields for Filtering[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#1-use-indexed-fields-for-filtering "Direct link to 1. Use Indexed Fields for Filtering")

**Indexed fields:**

*   `term_id` (primary keys)
*   `creator_id`
*   `type`
*   `created_at`
*   `account_id`

    # GOOD: Filtering by indexed fieldsquery GetAtoms($type: atom_type!, $since: timestamptz!) {  atoms(    where: {      type: { _eq: $type }      created_at: { _gte: $since }    }  ) {    term_id    label  }}

### 2\. Request Only Needed Fields[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#2-request-only-needed-fields "Direct link to 2. Request Only Needed Fields")

    # GOOD: Minimal field selectionquery GetAtoms {  atoms(limit: 10) {    term_id    label  }}

### 3\. Use Aggregates Instead of Fetching All Nodes[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#3-use-aggregates-instead-of-fetching-all-nodes "Direct link to 3. Use Aggregates Instead of Fetching All Nodes")

    # GOOD: Use aggregates for countsquery CountPositions($accountId: String!) {  positions_aggregate(where: { account_id: { _eq: $accountId } }) {    aggregate {      count    }  }}

### 4\. Limit Nested Queries[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#4-limit-nested-queries "Direct link to 4. Limit Nested Queries")

    # GOOD: Limit depth and breadthquery GetAtomWithTriples($id: String!) {  atom(term_id: $id) {    label    as_subject_triples(limit: 10) {      predicate { label }      object { label }    }  }}

### 5\. Use Primary Key Lookups When Possible[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#5-use-primary-key-lookups-when-possible "Direct link to 5. Use Primary Key Lookups When Possible")

    # GOOD: Direct primary key lookupquery GetAtom($id: String!) {  atom(term_id: $id) {    term_id    label  }}

### 6\. Paginate Large Result Sets[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#6-paginate-large-result-sets "Direct link to 6. Paginate Large Result Sets")

    # GOOD: Always use limitquery GetAtoms($limit: Int!, $offset: Int!) {  atoms(    limit: $limit    offset: $offset    order_by: { created_at: desc }  ) {    term_id    label  }}

### 7\. Use Pre-Computed Tables[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#7-use-pre-computed-tables "Direct link to 7. Use Pre-Computed Tables")

    # GOOD: Use time-series tablesquery GetDailyStats($termId: String!, $curveId: numeric!) {  share_price_change_stats_daily(    where: {      term_id: { _eq: $termId }      curve_id: { _eq: $curveId }    }    limit: 30  ) {    bucket    difference  }}

### 8\. Leverage Database Functions[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#8-leverage-database-functions "Direct link to 8. Leverage Database Functions")

    # GOOD: Use backend functionsquery GetFollowing($address: String!) {  following(args: { address: $address }) {    id    label  }}

Monitoring[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#monitoring "Direct link to Monitoring")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Profile query execution time
*   Monitor response payload sizes
*   Track cache hit rates
*   Watch for N+1 query patterns

*   [Performance Checklist](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#performance-checklist)
    *   [1\. Use Indexed Fields for Filtering](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#1-use-indexed-fields-for-filtering)
        
    *   [2\. Request Only Needed Fields](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#2-request-only-needed-fields)
        
    *   [3\. Use Aggregates Instead of Fetching All Nodes](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#3-use-aggregates-instead-of-fetching-all-nodes)
        
    *   [4\. Limit Nested Queries](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#4-limit-nested-queries)
        
    *   [5\. Use Primary Key Lookups When Possible](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#5-use-primary-key-lookups-when-possible)
        
    *   [6\. Paginate Large Result Sets](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#6-paginate-large-result-sets)
        
    *   [7\. Use Pre-Computed Tables](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#7-use-pre-computed-tables)
        
    *   [8\. Leverage Database Functions](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#8-leverage-database-functions)
        
*   [Monitoring](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/best-practices/performance#monitoring)

# Triples Pagination | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/triples-pagination#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Paginate through triples with total count for UI.

Query[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/triples-pagination#query "Direct link to Query")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Query

▶ Run Query

query GetTriplesPage($limit: Int!, $offset: Int!) {
  total: triples\_aggregate {
    aggregate {
      count
    }
  }
  triples(
    limit: $limit
    offset: $offset
    order\_by: { created\_at: desc }
  ) {
    term\_id
    created\_at
    subject { label image }
    predicate { label }
    object { label image }
  }
}

#### Variables

Click "Run Query" to execute the GraphQL query and see results

*   [Query](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/triples-pagination#query)

---

# User Positions | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/user-positions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Fetch user positions with aggregate statistics.

Query[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/user-positions#query "Direct link to Query")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Query

▶ Run Query

query GetUserPositions($accountId: String!, $limit: Int!) {
  stats: positions\_aggregate(where: { account\_id: { \_eq: $accountId } }) {
    aggregate {
      count
      sum { shares }
    }
  }
  positions(
    where: { account\_id: { \_eq: $accountId } }
    order\_by: { shares: desc }
    limit: $limit
  ) {
    id
    shares
    vault {
      term\_id
      current\_share\_price
      market\_cap
      term {
        atom { label image }
      }
    }
  }
}

#### Variables

Click "Run Query" to execute the GraphQL query and see results

*   [Query](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/examples/user-positions#query)

---

# Upload Image from URL | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image-from-url#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Upload an image by providing its URL. The server fetches, caches, and moderates the image. Returns a `CachedImage` with the hosted URL and safety score.

Mutation Structure[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image-from-url#mutation-structure "Direct link to Mutation Structure")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    mutation UploadImageFromUrl($image: UploadImageFromUrlInput!) {  uploadImageFromUrl(image: $image) {    images {      url      original_url      safe      score      model      created_at    }  }}

Variables[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image-from-url#variables "Direct link to Variables")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The mutation takes an `image` argument with the `UploadImageFromUrlInput` type:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `url` | `String` | Yes | URL of the image to upload |

    {  "image": {    "url": "https://example.com/images/logo.png"  }}

Response Fields[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image-from-url#response-fields "Direct link to Response Fields")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Returns `UploadImageFromUrlOutput` containing an `images` array of `CachedImage` objects:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `images` | `[CachedImage!]!` | Array of cached image results |
| `images[].url` | `String!` | Hosted image URL |
| `images[].original_url` | `String!` | Original source URL |
| `images[].safe` | `Boolean!` | Whether the image passed moderation |
| `images[].score` | `jsonb` | Moderation safety score details |
| `images[].model` | `String` | Moderation model used |
| `images[].created_at` | `timestamptz!` | Upload timestamp |

Expected Response[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image-from-url#expected-response "Direct link to Expected Response")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    {  "data": {    "uploadImageFromUrl": {      "images": [        {          "url": "https://cdn.example.com/images/abc123.png",          "original_url": "https://example.com/images/logo.png",          "safe": true,          "score": null,          "model": null,          "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"        }      ]    }  }}

Interactive Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image-from-url#interactive-example "Direct link to Interactive Example")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Query

▶ Run Query

mutation UploadImageFromUrl($image: UploadImageFromUrlInput!) {
  uploadImageFromUrl(image: $image) {
    images {
      url
      original\_url
      safe
    }
  }
}

#### Variables

Click "Run Query" to execute the GraphQL query and see results

Use Cases[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image-from-url#use-cases "Direct link to Use Cases")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Import External Images[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image-from-url#import-external-images "Direct link to Import External Images")

    import { GraphQLClient } from 'graphql-request'import { API_URL_PROD } from '@0xintuition/graphql'const client = new GraphQLClient(API_URL_PROD)async function importExternalImage(imageUrl: string) {  const mutation = `    mutation UploadImageFromUrl($image: UploadImageFromUrlInput!) {      uploadImageFromUrl(image: $image) {        images {          url          safe        }      }    }  `  const result = await client.request(mutation, {    image: { url: imageUrl }  })  const cached = result.uploadImageFromUrl.images[0]  if (!cached.safe) {    throw new Error('Image failed moderation check')  }  return cached.url}

Related[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/images/upload-image-from-url#related "Direct link to Related")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

     - Upload metadata
*   [Pin Person](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/graphql-api/mutations/pin-person)
     - Create Person with image

# Atom Events | Intuition Docs

[Skip to main content](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

Functions for parsing atom-related events from transaction receipts.

eventParseAtomCreated[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#eventparseatomcreated "Direct link to eventParseAtomCreated")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Parse AtomCreated events from a transaction.

### Parameters[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

| Parameter | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| publicClient | `PublicClient` | Viem public client | Yes |
| txHash | `Hash` | Transaction hash | Yes |

### Returns[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#returns "Direct link to Returns")

    Promise<Array<{  args: {    termId: bigint    termData: bytes    creatorAtomId: bigint  }  eventName: 'AtomCreated'  // ... other event fields}>>

### Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#example "Direct link to Example")

    import { eventParseAtomCreated } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const events = await eventParseAtomCreated(publicClient, txHash)events.forEach((event) => {  console.log('Atom ID:', event.args.termId)  console.log('Atom URI:', event.args.termData)  console.log('Creator:', event.args.creatorAtomId)})

* * *

eventParseAtomDepositCreated[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#eventparseatomdepositcreated "Direct link to eventParseAtomDepositCreated")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Parse AtomDepositCreated events (atom creation with initial deposit).

### Returns[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

    Promise<Array<{  args: {    termId: bigint    sender: Address    receiver: Address    assets: bigint    shares: bigint  }}>>

### Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#example-1 "Direct link to Example")

    import { eventParseAtomDepositCreated } from '@0xintuition/protocol'const events = await eventParseAtomDepositCreated(publicClient, txHash)events.forEach((event) => {  console.log('Atom ID:', event.args.termId)  console.log('Initial deposit:', formatEther(event.args.assets))  console.log('Shares received:', formatEther(event.args.shares))})

* * *

Complete Example[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#complete-example "Direct link to Complete Example")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    import {  multiVaultCreateAtoms,  eventParseAtomCreated,  eventParseDeposited,} from '@0xintuition/protocol'import { toHex, parseEther } from 'viem'// Create atom with depositconst atomCost = await multiVaultGetAtomCost({ address, publicClient })const deposit = atomCost + parseEther('1')const txHash = await multiVaultCreateAtoms(  { address, walletClient, publicClient },  {    args: [[toHex('My Atom')], [deposit]],    value: deposit,  })// Parse all eventsconst atomEvents = await eventParseAtomCreated(publicClient, txHash)const depositEvents = await eventParseDeposited(publicClient, txHash)console.log('Created atoms:', atomEvents.map(e => e.args.termId))console.log('Deposits:', depositEvents.map(e => formatEther(e.args.assets)))

* * *

See Also[​](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#see-also "Direct link to See Also")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Triple Events](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/triple-events)
    
*   [Vault Events](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/vault-events)
    
*   [Examples: Event Parsing](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/examples/event-parsing)
    

*   [eventParseAtomCreated](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#eventparseatomcreated)
    *   [Parameters](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#parameters)
        
    *   [Returns](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#returns)
        
    *   [Example](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#example)
        
*   [eventParseAtomDepositCreated](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#eventparseatomdepositcreated)
    *   [Returns](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#returns-1)
        
    *   [Example](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#example-1)
        
*   [Complete Example](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#complete-example)
    
*   [See Also](https://www.docs.intuition.systems/docs/protocol/events/atom-events#see-also)
